rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when bindings are implementation-specific, and into type scope only for port-level assumptions/guarantees.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by respecting component type vs. implementation scope boundaries for AGREE annex placement.
Do not assume AADL property names are directly visible as AGREE variables; only reference explicitly exposed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must use the exact syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Implementation-level bindings require subcomponents and connections to be declared before the annex.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties like Period, Priority, or Dispatch_Protocol as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data ports into numeric comparisons without explicit scalar typing.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
